composition_vocabulary: Predominantly isolated, centred subjects on high-contrast grounds — either stark product shots on plain white or black, or tight type specimens with generous negative space. Graphic-design tiles dominate; product tiles follow the same austere single-subject logic. Almost no layering or environmental context.
lighting_quality: Flat graphic/digital throughout; where physical product appears the lighting is clean, directionless studio — no chiaroscuro, no natural or atmospheric light anywhere in the sample.
material_textures: polished yellow gold, pavé diamond, smooth leather, skin, matte uncoated paper, crystal/rhinestone
aesthetic_register: A deliberate hybrid: heirloom-luxury anchored by the gold serpent jewellery, pulled hard toward contemporary brand-identity and streetwear-adjacent graphic design by the volume of logotype and wordmark tiles (STWD., SMRKTØ, Aimé Leon Dore, Saint Laurent script, Tabu).
notably_absent: No model wearing jewellery in situ, no soft editorial or lifestyle photography, no florals, no pearls, no pastel or blush tones — none of the conventionally feminine visual language that populates most luxury-jewellery moodboards.
